item.title: Finally finished this. So many problems.
item.description:
Our big laser is alive again!! For 3 MONTHS we couldn't get her going. Was pulling my hair out. This is the first piece we were able to make after the 3 months of down-time. If your a laser person, you understand how it can go... Thanks for looking. "Demure" 48" Round 12 Layers Hangs on a French Cleat Took 1 Billion hours to design and make (Approximation)
I also find "Demure" extremely difficult to photograph. Just the photo took me forever to get/edit. But, I'm happy with it.
Thanks for looking. If you want to see other things I make, check it out here: philip-roberts.com/ No cat tax. We have feral cats around the art studio that are cute and they keep their distance. I just found one in the nearest road and had to burry it about 20 minutes ago. Not a great morning.
item.dc:creator: PhilipRobertss

item.title: HERO!!
item.description: I didn't see anything about Mr. Evans yesterday (and nothing in the Imgur search that I did). I kind of feel that stories like this should be posted whenever they are seen.

item.dc:creator: Noonius

item.title: "We start talking to this young white lady, and she invites us to her room upstairs. We start making out on her bed when her roommate walks in. She starts yelling at the girl we were with: `You're having sex on my bed? You're a slut!'"
item.description: "From there, the reigns of racism took over. The young lady accused us of raping her. Going to court, facing an all-white jury, all I remember is how terrified my mom was. She said, `Baby, you don't understand. This ain't about the truth. This is about the reality of the America we live in.' "I was sentenced to 28 years in prison based off an accusation, and nothing more. My stomach is in knots just thinking about how easy it was for one accusation to be enough to turn my life upside down. I was 18 turning 19. I lost my innocence and youth that day. "Prison was a dark time for me, but I found strength from my mom. She couldn't afford to hire a lawyer for me, so she felt like it was her fault I was found guilty. "I wanted to rewrite what had been written about me. I started studying case law in the prison's library, and also helped other people with their cases — that was my crash course on law school. I wrote to people, and the Innocence Project answered. "We took the case to the Seventh Circuit Court of Appeals, and it was unanimously reversed. The case unraveled because we discovered that police withheld a statement from a college student, who said he saw us hanging out with our accuser before and after we went up to her room." "A more equitable criminal justice system looks like the diverse picture of America that we have. You can't have benches made up of 90% white former prosecutors. When I was standing in that courtroom with the all-white jury looking at me — we did not get the presumption of innocence. We went in with the historical stereotype of aggressive, ravaging, savaging Black boys. "One of the most significant cases I'm working on is US v. Claiborne. Two men, Ferrone Claiborne and Terence Richardson, were wrongfully charged with the murder of a police officer and sentenced to life — even though the jury found them not guilty. "The court had the opportunity to protect the Constitution or the police. And the court decided to protect the police. Protecting bad police erodes our faith in the entire police force. This country has grappled with qualified immunity, like with Breonna Taylor. We too often have judges and decisions that protect their positions and their party, but not the Constitution. "They call the prison system the Department of Corrections, but it really should be called the Department of Warehousing. Millions of Black men are incarcerated. Neighborhoods are suffering — it's like a leg missing from a chair, when you have that many men missing. If it really were a place for corrections, there would be change. But we're not providing mental healthcare or education to make sure people coming in and out of prison have the resources they need. We're warehousing them. "I think we need to treat things that ail our society as the pandemics they are: We have a pandemic of civil rights of Black folks in this world. We have a pandemic of women's rights, a pandemic when it comes to the justice system. We all need to stop and mobilize together and figure out how we get out of these pandemics together." https://www.insider.com/jarrett-adams-criminal-defense-lawyer-racial-justice-diversity-2022-7
item.dc:creator: MenloPart

item.title: Quitting smoking is a wild ride
item.description: Been a pack and a half a day for 17 years. Quitting because the pulmonologist thinks I might have COPD. No one told me how much everything smells. Food also tastes way too rich so I’m getting used to that
item.dc:creator: Ioftenpretendtobeapotato

item.title: YOU WANTED AN UPDATE IMGUR SO HERE IT IS
item.description: HELLO WORLD AND ALL WHO INHABIT IT! THIS IS YOUR NEWLY APPOINTED NOT-SINGLE CAPTAIN OF THE LOVE TRAIN SPEAKING. YESTERDAY's POST! https://imgur.com/gallery/wxOiUUe I DID IT. AND ALL OF YOU LOVELY PEOPLE WANTED A FOLLOW UP...WELL THIS IS IT! FIREWORKS ARE LITERALLY SHOOTING OUT OF MY BUTT RIGHT NOW I DONT KNOW WHAT TO DO. THIS IS MY FIRST FIRST FIRST DATE. IM 25 FOR GODS SAKE. I CAN'T STOP SMILING. SHE SAID YES.
item.dc:creator: Mickey421

item.title: Doozy
item.description:
Story Time: My ex can be super sweet, but when he gets mad, he scares me. He's on parole for a non-violent offense. He doesn't follow the rules but never seems to get caught. He uses drugs, leaves the state, and frequently fails to report things he's required to report. I kept waiting for him to get into trouble, but he's always one step ahead of his parole officer. The last straw was when he refused to leave my house. I called 911 and they just gave him a warning, instead giving me shit for letting him in. I am moving in a few weeks and I can't get myself to tell him he's not invited. He has very little money on account of his drug habit. He needs me to support him and gets massively angry if I am hesitant. I called his parole officer yesterday and spilled the beans. She thanked me for the information and then made an appointment with him for next week. He's all proud of himself for pulling the wool over her eyes. He doesn't know that he's looking at five to seven more years of prison. When it happens, I will be shocked, SHOCKED I tell you, and I will finally be free. TL;DR Ex is going back to prison because he's a user and a liar and I ratted him out. FP Edit: Thanks for all the incredible support and advice!!! I do have handguns for protection and I know how to use them. The doggo tax is not my doggo. I can register as a DV victim after he's arrested and that will automatically allow for a protection order once he gets out. He has no idea about what's going to happen at his next appointment. In fact, he was laughing about it, saying that he knows he isn't in trouble. I do plan on ghosting him as soon as he goes away and I know the arrest will happen. Finally, I changed some details so he won't recognize this as being about him if he happens to see it but he doesn't do Imgur.
doggo tax
item.dc:creator: IUsedToBeGlorious

item.title: I'm tired
item.description:
I am beyond tired with life. Tired of christofascism stomping its way through my nation. Tired of my friends being judged solely by the color of their skin or sexual orientation. Tired of such a blah and pointless existence. Tired of man babies who get their fee-fees hurtwhen they are called out for supporting fascists. So fucking tired and I see no hope for the future.
Pretty much. I will get through.
item.dc:creator: Emiloo74

item.title: Vote Damn It!
item.description:
EVERY election counts now. From your local school board to the governor, it COUNTS! Vote like our lives depend on it, because they do. More and more people are being sucked into conspiracies because it's easier to accept than to think. Being intelligent and having original and critical thoughts are being looked down on. We need less thoughts and prayers and more critical thinking.
Cat tax. Fat Boy and Little Girl Saucignon: https://twitter.com/DavidYankovich/status/1544464743271915522?t=GXhbA4O2G5H3qJTy7ky0pQ&s=19
item.dc:creator: jessiPiJunkie

item.title: It sucks with so much going on, this stays in the shadow
item.description: I get the powerful have the leverage, but man the attention that Johnny Depp v Bed Pooper got should be just as equal to this. This is not to dimiss the light the Depp brought to us, but makes me scratch my head on how something this important, if not more, doesnt get the attention in needs. Just sucks with so much going on these days, are time is split.
